title: pellaea atropurpurea f. glabella
intro: Pellaea atropurpurea f. glabella, also known as the glabrous leather fern, is a variant of the Pellaea atropurpurea plant, a type of fern found in North America. The plant is primarily known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds that can be used to treat wounds and skin irritations. The health benefits of Pellaea atropurpurea f. glabella include its ability to reduce swelling, promote tissue repair, and act as an antiseptic to prevent infection, making it a valuable asset in natural remedies for wound care. Pellaea atropurpurea f. glabella can be prepared into various herbal remedies, including teas, infusions, and salves, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
